subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 4 players.
- **Scaling** — Number of Places of Power and monuments available varies by player count.
- **Solo mode** — Not included in the base game.

#### Cards
- **Artifact cards** — 40 artifact cards (the core engine-building cards).
- **Mage cards** — 10 Mage cards (each with a unique starting ability).
- **Magic Item cards** — 8 Magic Items (powerful one-use-per-round effects).
- **Monument cards** — 10 Monument cards (purchasable VP sources).
- **Place of Power tiles** — 5 double-sided Place of Power tiles (10 total options, claim for VP and abilities).

#### Resources (Essences)
- **Gold essences** — Gold tokens.
- **Elan essences** — Red tokens (fiery energy).
- **Calm essences** — Green tokens (life energy).
- **Death essences** — Black tokens (dark energy).
- **Life essences** — Blue tokens (water/life energy).
- **Wild token** — Can substitute for any essence type.

#### Other Components
- **VP tokens** — Victory point tracking tokens.
- **Player reference cards** — Summary cards for each player.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
1. **Place of Power tiles** — Select Places of Power based on player count and lay them in the center.
2. **Monument display** — Shuffle monuments and reveal a number based on player count.
3. **Magic Items** — Lay out all 8 Magic Item cards face up.
4. **Essence supply** — Create a common pool of all essence tokens.
5. **VP tokens** — Place VP tokens in a central supply.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Places of Power** — Use 5 Places of Power (one side of each tile, chosen or random).
- **Monuments** — Use a reduced monument display.

--- 3-4 Players ---
- **Places of Power** — Use 5 Places of Power.
- **Monuments** — Use the full monument display.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Draft Phase
- **Deal artifacts** — Deal 8 artifact cards to each player.
- **Draft method** — Players draft artifacts: choose 1 card, pass the rest. Repeat until all 8 are kept.
- **Simplified variant** — For first games, deal 8 cards and keep all (no drafting).

#### Mage and Magic Item Selection
- **Deal Mages** — Deal 2 Mage cards to each player; choose 1 and discard the other.
- **Starting Magic Item** — Each player selects 1 Magic Item from the display.
- **Mage placement** — Place your Mage face up in front of you.

#### Starting Resources
- **Starting essences** — Collect any starting essences shown on your Mage card.
- **Starting hand** — Your 8 drafted artifacts form your personal deck; draw 3 as your opening hand.
- **Remaining artifacts** — Keep the other 5 face down as your draw pile.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Beginning Play
- **First player** — Determine randomly.
- **Turn order** — Clockwise from first player.
- **Initial state** — Each player has: 1 Mage in play, 1 Magic Item, 3 artifact cards in hand, 5 artifacts in draw pile, starting essences per Mage.
- **Goal reminder** — First player to reach 10+ VP at the end of a round triggers the final check.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round Structure
1. **Collect essences** — Each player simultaneously collects essences from their Mage, artifacts in play, and Places of Power they own.
2. **Action phase** — Players take turns performing one action at a time, going clockwise.
3. **Pass** — When a player cannot or does not want to act, they pass and select a new Magic Item for next round.
4. **Check victory** — After all players pass, check if any player has 10+ VP.

#### Action Options (One Per Turn)
- **Place an artifact** — Pay its essence cost and put it into play from your hand.
- **Use a power** — Activate an ability on an artifact, Mage, Place of Power, or Magic Item (tap to use).
- **Discard for essences** — Discard an artifact card from hand to gain 1 Gold essence and 1 other essence of your choice.
- **Claim a Place of Power** — Pay the required essences to claim an unclaimed Place of Power.
- **Buy a monument** — Pay 4 Gold to purchase a monument (worth VP).
- **Pass** — Take a new Magic Item and stop acting this round.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Placing Artifacts
- **Cost** — Pay the essences shown in the top-left corner of the artifact card.
- **In play** — Once placed, the artifact stays in play and generates essences each round.
- **Abilities** — Some artifacts have tap abilities (use once per round) or triggered effects.

#### Places of Power
- **Claim cost** — Each Place of Power has a specific essence cost to claim.
- **VP value** — Places of Power are worth VP as printed.
- **Ongoing abilities** — Most Places of Power also provide essence generation or conversion abilities.
- **One per player** — You can claim multiple Places of Power over the game.

#### Monuments
- **Cost** — 4 Gold essences each.
- **VP value** — Each monument is worth a fixed VP amount (typically 1-2 VP).
- **Limited supply** — Once a monument is bought, it is gone.

#### Magic Items
- **One-time per round** — Each Magic Item can be used once per round.
- **Swap on pass** — When you pass, you return your Magic Item and select a new one for next round.
- **Abilities vary** — Effects range from essence conversion to attack to card draw.

#### Attacking
- **Dragon attack** — Some cards and abilities let you attack opponents, forcing them to discard essences.
- **Defense** — Shields on artifacts and Places of Power can absorb attacks.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Essence Economy
- **Tight resources** — The economy is deliberately constrained; every essence matters.
- **Conversion** — Many abilities convert one essence type to another.
- **Gold versatility** — Gold is the most flexible essence, used for monuments and many costs.
- **Discard for essences** — Discarding a card from hand gives 1 Gold + 1 essence of choice, useful in emergencies.

#### Mage Abilities
- **Unique powers** — Each Mage provides a different starting ability and sometimes starting essences.
- **Draft synergy** — Draft artifacts that complement your Mage's strengths.
- **Examples** — The Necromancer synergizes with Death essences; the Alchemist converts essences efficiently.

#### Passing and Turn Order
- **First to pass** — The first player to pass gets first pick of Magic Items for next round.
- **Strategic pass** — Passing early gets better Magic Items but loses remaining actions.
- **Late pass** — Staying in lets you take more actions but gives worse Magic Item selection.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **VP threshold** — At the end of any round, if one or more players have 10+ VP, the game ends.
- **Final round** — All players complete the round before checking.

#### VP Sources
- **Places of Power** — VP printed on each claimed Place of Power.
- **Monuments** — VP printed on each purchased monument.
- **Artifact VP** — Some artifacts have VP printed on them.
- **Gold conversion** — Each Gold essence remaining converts to VP at a rate specified by game state.

#### Winning
- **Highest VP** — The player with the most VP wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — Player with the most total essences remaining breaks ties.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Draft for synergy** — During the draft, focus on artifacts that share essence types and combo with your Mage.
- **Engine before VP** — Prioritize artifacts that generate essences over those that score points in the first few rounds.
- **Identify your path** — Decide early whether you are racing toward Places of Power, monuments, or artifact VP.
- **Magic Item selection** — Choose a Magic Item that fills a gap in your essence production.
- **Tempo awareness** — Someone will reach 10 VP faster than you expect; build quickly.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Over-drafting expensive artifacts** — Keeping too many high-cost artifacts creates hands you cannot afford to play.
- **Ignoring Places of Power** — Places of Power provide the most VP per action and should not be overlooked.
- **Hoarding essences** — Sitting on unspent essences while opponents build their engines wastes tempo.
- **Discarding too many cards** — Using discard-for-essences too often depletes your artifact options.
- **Undervaluing Gold** — Gold is needed for monuments and is the most convertible essence.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Place of Power vs Monuments
- **Places of Power** — High VP but expensive and limited; require specific essence types.
- **Monuments** — Flexible (just Gold) but lower individual VP value.

#### Decision 2: When to Pass
- **Pass early** — Secure the best Magic Item for next round at the cost of fewer actions.
- **Pass late** — Maximize actions this round but accept leftover Magic Items.

#### Decision 3: Play Artifacts vs Discard for Essences
- **Play** — Build your engine and future generation capacity.
- **Discard** — Gain immediate essences for a crucial purchase this turn.
